title: Anopsia
body: An anopsia, or anopia, (from Ancient Greek  ἀν- (an-) 'without' and  ὄψις (opsis) 'sight') is a defect in the visual field. If the defect is only partial, then the portion of the field with the defect can be used to isolate the underlying cause. It is particularly used to describe the lack of sight in one eye.
categories: Articles with unsourced statements from October 2025
